questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of ethnocentrism?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Feeling poor despite a solid income because one's neighbors are far wealthier.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sweeping public alarm that a new video game is corrupting the nation's youth.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immigrant families adopting the dominant language and customs over generations until differences fade.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Assuming that eating with one's hands instead of utensils is simply backward and wrong.

questions.explanation: An example of ethnocentrism is: assuming that eating with one's hands instead of utensils is simply backward and wrong. In general, ethnocentrism refers to the tendency to evaluate other cultures by the standards of one's own culture, seen as superior. The other options illustrate different concepts.

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of the fight-or-flight response?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The draining pressure of demands that feel beyond one's control.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A worker staying engaged when given choice, skill-building, and connection.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A pounding heart and tensed muscles readying a person to run from danger.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Feeling fear and a pounding heart at the very same instant when facing danger.

questions.explanation: An example of the fight-or-flight response is: a pounding heart and tensed muscles readying a person to run from danger. In general, the fight-or-flight response refers to the body's rapid physiological mobilization to confront or flee a threat. The other options illustrate different concepts.

choices.label: C
